title: eupomatales
intro: Eupomatales is an order of flowering plants that consists of approximately 9 families of aquatic and terrestrial plants. It is characterized by the presence of flowers with three or more sepals and usually three petals, and often showy inflorescences. Families within the Eupomatales order include the Orchidaceae, Euphorbiaceae, and Cyperaceae. These families are found in various habitats, including wetlands and tropical regions, and are often known for their unique adaptations to their environments.

families.family_desc: Eupomatiaceae is a family of flowering plants in the order Laurales, consisting of about 11 species of evergreen trees native to New Guinea and nearby islands.
